title: Declaring & Calling
description: How to create a custom function and run it.

slides.title: Declaring a Function 🗣️
slides.content:
To create (declare) a function, specify the name of the function, followed by parentheses `()`.

`void myFunction() { ... }`

slides.title: Void 🕳️
slides.content: `void` means that the function does not have a return value. You will learn more about return values later.

slides.title: Calling the Function
slides.content: To use it, you must 'call' it inside main().

quizData.question: What will the output of the code above be?
quizData.explanation: The function is called twice in main, so its inner code `cout << ...` runs exactly two times.

slides.title: Above main() ⚠️
slides.content:
In C++, a user-defined function MUST be declared BEFORE the `main()` function.

If you try to declare your custom function *below* `main()`, the compiler won't know it exists when you try to call it!

quizData.question: Where should you write your custom functions like `void myFunction()` in C++?
quizData.explanation: Custom functions should be declared and defined before the main() block so main() is aware of them.
